chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّ الْجِنَّ يَأْتِيهِمْ فَيَسْأَلُونَهُمْ عَنْ مَعَالِمِ دِينِهِمْ وَ يَتَوَجَّهُونَ فِي أُمُورِهِمْ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 98 – The Jinn come to them (Imams<sup>asws</sup>), so they are asking them<sup>asws</sup> about the information of their Religion, and they are betaking to their orders

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (295/ 4)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (295/ 4)
text: بَيْنَا أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) عَلَى الْمِنْبَرِ إِذْ أَقْبَلَ ثُعْبَانٌ مِنْ نَاحِيَةِ بَابٍ مِنْ أَبْوَابِ الْمَسْجِدِ فَهَمَّ النَّاسُ أَنْ يَقْتُلُوهُ فَأَرْسَلَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) أَنْ كُفُّوا فَكَفُّوا وَ أَقْبَلَ الثُّعْبَانُ يَنْسَابُ حَتَّى انْتَهَى إِلَى الْمِنْبَرِ فَتَطَاوَلَ فَسَلَّمَ عَلَى أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فَأَشَارَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) إِلَيْهِ أَنْ يَقِفَ حَتَّى يَفْرُغَ مِنْ خُطْبَتِهِ

وَ لَمَّا فَرَغَ مِنْ خُطْبَتِهِ أَقْبَلَ عَلَيْهِ فَقَالَ مَنْ أَنْتَ فَقَالَ عَمْرُو بْنُ عُثْمَانَ خَلِيفَتِكَ عَلَى الْجِنِّ وَ إِنَّ أَبِي مَاتَ وَ أَوْصَانِي أَنْ آتِيَكَ فَأَسْتَطْلِعَ رَأْيَكَ وَ قَدْ أَتَيْتُكَ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فَمَا تَأْمُرُنِي بِهِ وَ مَا تَرَى

فَقَالَ لَهُ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) أُوصِيكَ بِتَقْوَى اللَّهِ وَ أَنْ تَنْصَرِفَ فَتَقُومَ مَقَامَ أَبِيكَ فِي الْجِنِّ فَإِنَّكَ خَلِيفَتِي عَلَيْهِمْ قَالَ فَوَدَّعَ عَمْرٌو أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَ انْصَرَفَ فَهُوَ خَلِيفَتُهُ عَلَى الْجِنِّ
فَقُلْتُ لَهُ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ فَيَأْتِيكَ عَمْرٌو وَ ذَاكَ الْوَاجِبُ عَلَيْهِ قَالَ نَعَمْ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya and Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Al Hassan, from Ibrahim Bin Hashim, from Amro Bin Usman, from Ibrahim Bin Ayoub, from Amro Bin Shimr, from Jabir,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘While Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> was upon the Pulpit, a snake came over from the direction of a door from the doors of the Masjid. So the people thought of killing it, but Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> sent a message that they should refrain. So they refrained, and the snake glided until it ended up to the Pulpit, and stood elongated and greeted upon Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>. But, Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> gestured towards it that it should pause until he<sup>asws</sup> is free from his<sup>asws</sup> sermon.

And when he<sup>asws</sup> was free from his<sup>asws</sup> sermon, he<sup>asws</sup> turned towards it and he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Who are you?’ So it said, ‘Amro Bin Usman, your Caliph upon the Jinn, and that my father died and bequeathed me that I should come over to you and find out your<sup>asws</sup> view, and so I have come to you, O Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>! So what are you<sup>asws</sup> ordering me with, and what is your<sup>asws</sup> view?’

So Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> said to it: ‘I<sup>asws</sup> command you with the fear of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, and when you leave, so you should stand in the place of your father among the Jinn, for you are (now) my<sup>asws</sup> Caliph upon them’. So Amro bade farewell to Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> and left, and he was his<sup>asws</sup> Caliph upon the Jinn’.
So I said to him<sup>asws</sup>, ‘May I be sacrificed for you<sup>asws</sup>! So does Amro come to you, and is that An Obligation upon him?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Yes’.
en.sarwar: 6. Muhammad ibn Yahya and Ahmad ibn Muhammad have narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Hassan from Ibrahim ibn Hashim from ‘Amr ibn ‘Uthman from Ibrahim ibn Ayyub from ‘Amr ibn Shimr from Jabir from abu Ja‘far (a.s.) who has said the following. “Once when Amir al-Mu’minin (a.s.) was on the pulpit a serpent entered from one of the doors of the mosque. People wanted to kill him. Amir al-Mu’minin (a.s.) asked them to leave him alone. They left him alone. The serpent began to crawl to the pulpit. He stretched and saluted to Amir al-Mu’minin (a.s.). Amir al-Mu’minin (a.s.) made a gesture to him to wait until the sermon is completed. When the sermon was completed he came to him and said, “Who are you?” He said, “I am ‘Amr ibn ‘Uthman, your deputy among the Jinns. My father has died and he has asked me in his will to come to you and find out your instructions and that is why I am here. What do you command me to do and what do you see.” Amir al-Mu’minin (a.s.) said, “I ask you to be pious before Allah and go back and act as the successor of your father among the Jinns. I appoint you as my deputy over them.” The narrator has said that ‘Amr then said farewell to Amir al-Mu’minin (a.s.) and left as his deputy over the Jinns. I asked The Imam (a.s.), may Allah take my soul in service for your cause, does ‘Amr fulfill that obligation on him?” He said, “Yes, he does so.”
